changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "To Hinamizawa"

TextENGNew: ``Ah, there he is.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3002. Heeey, Arakawa-chan.``
I looked up from the evening tabloid I had picked up from the convenience store and turned back. The voice came from a colleague of mine, Mishima in the sports department.
``Hey, Mishima-san, glad you could make it.@k@|@y@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3003. ...Huh?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3004. Weren't you supposed to be in Seattle all week?``
``Yeah, that was the plan.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3004. But I actually forgot to update my passport... Tahaha.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3005. So, they ended up sending Yoshimura instead.``
``Huh? Yoshimura?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3006. His English is not that great... Will he be all right?``
``Well, nothing to worry about.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3007. I told him to record all of his interviews.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3008. And then we can go over the transcripts together here.``
``I wonder if that'll be ok...``
I was actually more concerned about Yoshimura's interviewing ability than his language skills.
If he just goes through the list of questions top to bottom, we won't get to hear any interesting material.
...Although, with sports articles you can get a decent amount of traction just from dropping the names and photos of famous players.
I haven't yet become desperate enough to request a position like that. ...But I am envious.
``By the way, I'll be in Osaka tomorrow.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3010. Kameda is getting close to his 200th win, and I'd like to ask about it.``

``Kameda... huh?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3009. The ``blazing fastball pitcher of tragedy`` is still perfecting his art.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3010. He tends to fall apart if his control gives out, so it gives me the chills just watching him.``
``Anyway, I saw this month's ``Ghostly Village Special Edition!``@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3012. It was packed with all kinds of long lost topics, like that Hinamizawa village~!``
``Haha, long lost topics? ...That was practically my life's work.``
Anyway, this guy was the one who went through my numerous occult materials and boiled them down to the most interesting topics.
Plus, he contributed some topics he found on the net from time to time -- even some material I didn't know myself.
So, there's no point feeling worn out by good news now.
``It was back when I was in school, probably 20 years ago?@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3014. It was a major uproar at the time.``
``The Great Hinamizawa Disaster.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3013. I saw it on TV, too.``
Poisonous volcanic gas emerged from a swamp near a remote village in the Chubu district, killing over 2,000 villagers overnight... and it came to be known as the ``Great Hinamizawa Disaster.``
Not only was that an incredible shock, but one after another, former residents of Hinamizawa began dying in strange incidents.
People committing murder-suicides against their entire families.
People committing murder-suicides against strangers and acquaintances.
In all cases, the people committing these heinous acts were otherwise normal people living peaceful lives.
And so, discrimination against people from Hinamizawa developed into a serious social conflict, significant enough that the national government had to step in.
And, all the people involved in those incidents said the exact same thing.
``This wasn't a natural disaster.@k It was the curse of Oyashiro-sama...``
And they weren't all just reckless, baseless rumors spread by total strangers.
In fact, my father also...
``Eerie, huh? ...Such an odd story.@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3016. But unlike you, I don't believe in ghosts.``
``Haha, big words to be telling an occult writer.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3015. ...But in recent years, people on the net have been posting a lot of theories that have nothing to do with curses.``
For example: ``The gas disaster was a government conspiracy.``
And: ``The village was blockaded to conceal an alien invasion.``
Not one of them had any concrete evidence. Baseless speculation. But even so, that didn't stop the occult rumors regarding Hinamizawa from piling up.
``Some of them made you immediately shout, ``No way,`` but others sounded believable.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3017. As a supporter of occult explanations, I feel I ought to try to verify it.``
``Wow...@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3018. Verify it? ...No way, you?``
There it was, at last.
Then Mishima-san noticed the knapsack lying at the base of my desk, and gave me an uneasy look.
``Yes, it's exactly what you think it is.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3019. Anyway, I figured I'd go to see if I can find anything out.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3020. So, I'm heading to Hinamizawa tomorrow.``
``Whaaat?!@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3020. T-tomorroooow?!``
I understood why Mishima-san was so surprised.
I was planning to head there in June to work on my article, so he must've immediately thought, ``That's dangerous. The disaster happened in June...``
``W-wait a minute!@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3022. You're really telling me you're going? Don't you know what month it is?!@k@vS20/00/VTT_mishima3023. Don't blame me if something happens to you!!``
``What are you talking about?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3022. You just said you don't believe in ghosts, Mishima-san. This is unlike you.``
``Besides, as an occult writer seeking new stories, doesn't it make sense to go where something is likely to happen?``
``W-well, I suppose that's true...``
``Anyway, I've gotten the editor-in-chief's approval, and my expenses will be covered.``
``Still, in the unlikely event that I ended up getting ``demoned away`` and never returned, I told him he should make a story out of it...``
``Not funny...``
``...Whoops, guess it's already time.@k@|@y@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3027. I've got a lot of preparations to do for tomorrow, so I'm heading out for the day.``
``A-Arakawa-chan...!``
Mishima-san waved his arms in a panic as he watched me head out of the editorial department.
Although the gas disaster killed over 2,000 villagers, it was the over 20-year blockade of Hinamizawa village that turned it into a ghost town...
It was just as Mishima-san said. I honestly had my doubts that any ghosts would actually appear.
But there may still be things worth investigating about this ghost town.
And there might even be some paranormal phenomenon at play that I simply couldn't grasp using the data I've gathered thus far...
``...Well, there's no way to know what may happen.@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3029. After all, only the gods may know that.``
...Come to think of it, I did hear that demons lived in Hinamizawa in ancient times.
With that in mind, nothing else would surprise me.

The next moment, the cell phone in the back pocket of my jeans started to ring. When I looked at the screen, I found the call was from the editor-in-chief of the Weekly Seven, who was out all day today.
That's unusual... the editor-in-chief doesn't normally contact me directly.
``Yes, hello?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3032. ...Huh? A travel companion?@k@vS20/62/VTT_arakawa3033. Umm, well, I guess that's all right...``
